title: Conformal Invariance of the large-$N$ limit of the $O(N)$ universality class
authors: Santiago Cabrera, Gonzalo De Polsi, Adam Rançon, Nicolás Wschebor
abstract: Conformal symmetry is expected to be realized in many equilibrium statistical mechanical systems at criticality. Although this is certainly true in two-dimensional systems, the three-dimensional case is subtler, and only a few proofs exist, only so in very specific cases. In this work, we give two proofs for the large $N$ limit of the $O(N)$ universality class within the non-perturbative renormalization group framework: one functional, and one vertex-by-vertex in Fourier space. While doing so, we unveil how the theory is structured in order for conformal symmetry to be realized. As a consequence, we shed light on what to expect, on rather general grounds, for a theory to be conformally invariant.
summary: This paper provides a rigorous mathematical proof that the large-N limit of the O(N) universality class possesses full conformal symmetry. Using the non-perturbative renormalization group framework, the authors show that the scale-invariant Wilson-Fisher fixed point satisfies the necessary Ward identities for special conformal transformations. The work clarifies how the theory's structure reorganizes to realize this symmetry and provides general insights into the conditions for symmetry enhancement at criticality.
main_problem: The paper investigates whether scale invariance automatically enhances to full conformal invariance at renormalization group fixed points in dimensions d > 2, specifically for the O(N) universality class.
main_result: The authors provide two rigorous proofs—one functional and one vertex-by-vertex—demonstrating that the large-N limit of the O(N) universality class is conformally invariant at the Wilson-Fisher fixed point.
method: The study utilizes the Non-Perturbative Renormalization Group (NPRG) framework, employing Legendre transforms and analyzing modified Ward identities induced by an infrared regulator.
model_or_system: The model is the O(N)-symmetric scalar phi^4 theory, which includes universality classes such as the Ising, XY, and Heisenberg models, analyzed in the large-N limit.
key_observables: Correlation functions, critical exponents (such as nu), scaling dimensions (D_phi, D_sigma), and the energy-momentum tensor.
important_parameters: Dimension d (2 < d < 4), the large-N limit, the renormalization group scale k, and the vanishing anomalous dimension (eta = 0).
main_assumptions: The proofs are specifically restricted to the large-N limit where the anomalous dimension eta is zero, and they assume the system is at the Wilson-Fisher fixed point.
figures_summary: The figures consist of diagrammatic representations of various vertex functions (H2, H3, gamma4) and their constituent loop channels and momentum-dependent contributions.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of conformal symmetry in d > 2, establishes the NPRG framework and modified Ward identities, presents a functional proof of conformal invariance, provides a vertex-by-vertex proof in Fourier space, and concludes with detailed mathematical appendices verifying the algebraic identities.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks

figures.caption: FIG. 2. Diagrammatic representation of two contributions to ˆΓ(4) vertex. Plain lines represent propagators. Integration over the loop is assumed. Dashed lines with a dot represent ˆΓ(2) vertices. The loop parts are equal to I(3) or I(4). Signs and factors N are omitted (see Eq. (42)). For each diagram, there are two possible extra permutations of momenta.
FIG. 4. Independent diagrams contributing to the only channel in H(3), where the continuous internal lines correspond to propagators given in Eq. (64) and vertices are just 1.

FIG. 5. Independent diagrams contributing to H(4), where the continuous internal lines correspond to propagators given in Eq .(64) and vertices are just 1. Notice that in every column, diagrams are equivalent up to a cyclic permutation of the explicit external momenta (p1, p2, p3), while diagrams within a row are equivalent up to a cyclic rotation of all external momenta. We thus refer to each row of diagrams as a different “channel”.
